pattern: Each building can rise to the smaller of its row's tallest and its column's tallest, so precomputing those two maxima turns the whole answer into one sweep.
hints: A building's height can grow only until it would poke above a silhouette, and there are two silhouettes that constrain it.
For every cell the ceiling is the minimum of the maximum in its row and the maximum in its column; the gain is that ceiling minus the current height.
Precompute each row maximum and each column maximum, then sum min(rowMax[r], colMax[c]) - grid[r][c] over all cells.

edge_inputs.description: mixed heights with room to grow on the interior
all zeros leave no room so the gain is zero
smallest grid of uniform height cannot grow
single tall corner caps everything else low
a row of zeros forces that row to stay flat
every value distinct so each cell has a unique ceiling
two by two with maximum values everywhere

stress.description: random square grid over the full value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, max(2, min(size, 50)))
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 100) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: square shape enforced so rows and columns line up
